241   safe_snprintf(combined_buf5, sizeof(combined_buf5), "%s --discovery-service --discovery-service-port 27225",
242                 example_buf2);
243 
244   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_BINARY, combined_buf1, "Join session and stream from local video file",
245                               false);
246   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_BINARY, combined_buf2, "Join session and stream from YouTube video",
247                               false);
248   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_BINARY, combined_buf3, "Join session and stream media from stdin", false);
249   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_BINARY, combined_buf4, "Join session with custom ASCII palette characters",
250                               false);
251   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_BINARY, combined_buf5, "Join session via custom discovery server", false);
252 
253   // Add examples for server-like modes (server + discovery-service)
254   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_SERVER_LIKE, NULL, "Start on localhost (127.0.0.1 and ::1)", false);
255   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_SERVER_LIKE, "0.0.0.0", "Start on all IPv4 interfaces", false);
256   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_SERVER_LIKE,
257                               "0.0.0.0 ::", "Start on all IPv4 and IPv6 interfaces (dual-stack)", false);
258   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_SERVER_LIKE, "--port 8080", "Start on custom port", false);
259 
260   // Server-specific examples
261   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_SERVER, "--key ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 --discovery",
262                               "Start with identity key and discovery registration", false);
263 
264   // Add examples for client-like modes (client + mirror)
265   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_CLIENT_LIKE, "--url 'https://youtu.be/7ynHVGCehoM'",
266                               "Stream from YouTube URL (also supports RTSP, HTTP, and HTTPS URLs)", false);
267   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_CLIENT_LIKE, "--url 'https://www.twitch.tv/ludwig'",
268                               "Stream Ludwig from videogames on Twitch", false);
269   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_CLIENT_LIKE, "-f video.mp4", "Stream from local video file", false);
270   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_CLIENT_LIKE, "--palette-chars '@%#*+=-:. '",
271                               "Custom palette characters to use. UTF-8 is allowed.", false);
272   options_builder_add_example(
273       b, OPTION_MODE_CLIENT_LIKE, "--snapshot",
274       "Print ascii art for --snapshot-delay's value of seconds then print the last frame and exit. "
275       "In snapshot mode, --width, --height, and --color are NOT autodetected when piping stdin in or redirecting "
276       "output.",
277       false);
278   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_CLIENT_LIKE, "--color-filter cyan --palette cool",
279                               "Apply cyan color filter and cool palette", false);
280 
281   // Client-specific examples
282   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_CLIENT, "example.com", "Connect to remote server", false);
283   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_CLIENT, "example.com:8080", "Connect to remote server on custom port",
284                               false);
285   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_CLIENT, "--color-mode mono --render-mode half-block --width 120",
286                               "Connect with custom display options", false);
287 
288   // Mirror-specific examples (unique to mirror mode)
289   options_builder_add_example(
290       b, OPTION_MODE_MIRROR, NULL,
291       "View the webcam or files or URLs as ASCII art. Like client mode but without network connectivity or a server.",
292       false);
293   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_MIRROR, "--color-mode mono", "View webcam in black and white", false);
294   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_MIRROR, "--color-filter green",
295                               "View webcam with green monochromatic color filter", false);
296   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_MIRROR, "--matrix --color-filter rainbow",
297                               "Matrix rain effect with rainbow colors cycling over 3.5s", false);
298   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_MIRROR, "--file '-'",
299                               "Stream media from stdin (cat file.gif | ascii-chat mirror -f '-')", false);
300   options_builder_add_example_utility(b, OPTION_MODE_MIRROR, "cat video.avi | ascii-chat mirror -f '-' -l -s 00:30",
301                                       "Stream .avi from stdin, looped, seeking to 00:30", true);
302   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_MIRROR, "--file video.mov --seek 22:10",
303                               "Start playback at exactly 22:10 (also works with --url)", false);
304   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_MIRROR, "-f 'https://youtu.be/LS9W8SO-Two' -S -D 0 -s 5:12",
305                               "Print a single frame from a YouTube video at exactly 5:12 and exit", false);
306   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_MIRROR, "-S -D 0 | tee frame.txt | pbcopy",
307                               "Capture single ASCII frame to clipboard (macOS) and file", false);
308   options_builder_add_example_utility(b, OPTION_MODE_MIRROR, "pbpaste | cat -",
309                                       "View ASCII frame from clipboard (macOS)", true);
310 
311   // Discovery-service specific examples
312   options_builder_add_example(b, OPTION_MODE_DISCOVERY_SVC, "--require-server-identity --require-client-identity",
313                               "Enforce identity verification for all parties", false);
314 
315   // Add mode descriptions
316   options_builder_add_mode(b, "default", "When the ascii-chat binary is used without a mode, it operates as either client or server automatically and uses the discovery-service to make peer connections with session strings");
317   options_builder_add_mode(b, "server", "Run as multi-client video chat server");
318   options_builder_add_mode(b, "client", "Run as video chat client (connect to server)");
319   options_builder_add_mode(b, "mirror", "View local media as ASCII art (no server)");
320   options_builder_add_mode(b, "discovery-service", "Secure P2P session signalling");
321 
322   // Add custom help sections for interactive modes (client, mirror, and discovery)
323   options_builder_add_custom_section(b, "KEYBINDINGS",
324                                      "Available in ascii-chat client, mirror, and discovery modes. "
325                                      "While rendering, press '?' to display a keyboard shortcuts help menu showing:\n"
326                                      "  - Available keybindings (?, Space, arrows, m, c, f, r)\n"
327                                      "  - Current settings (volume, color mode, audio status, etc.)",
328                                      OPTION_MODE_CLIENT_LIKE);
329 
330   // Add environment variables section (all modes)
331   options_builder_add_custom_section(
332       b, "ENVIRONMENT",
333       "All command-line flags that accept values have corresponding environment variables.\n"
334       "  Format: ASCII_CHAT_<FLAG_NAME> where FLAG_NAME is uppercase with hyphens replaced by underscores\n"
335       "  Example: --color-filter maps to ASCII_CHAT_COLOR_FILTER\n"
336       "\n"
337       "  Configuration precedence (lowest to highest):\n"
338       "    1. Config file values (~/.ascii-chat/config.toml)\n"
339       "    2. Environment variables (ASCII_CHAT_*)\n"
340       "    3. Command-line flags (--flag-name)\n"
341       "\n"
342       "  Additional environment variables are documented in the ascii-chat(1) man page.",
343       OPTION_MODE_ALL);
344 
345   // Add common dependencies (these will be validated after parsing)
346   // Note: Dependencies are validated at runtime, so we add them here for documentation
347 
348   // ============================================================================
349   // Media Source Conflicts
350   // ============================================================================
351   // URL conflicts: --url cannot be used with --file or --loop
352   options_builder_add_dependency_conflicts(b, "url", "file",
353                                            "Option --url cannot be used with --file (--url takes priority)");
354   options_builder_add_dependency_conflicts(
355       b, "url", "loop", "Option --url cannot be used with --loop (network streams cannot be looped)");
356 
357   // ============================================================================
358   // Encryption & Authentication Conflicts
359   // ============================================================================
360   // Cannot use both --encrypt and --no-encrypt
361   options_builder_add_dependency_conflicts(b, "no-encrypt", "encrypt", "Cannot use --no-encrypt with --encrypt");
362 
363   // Cannot use --no-auth with authentication material (--key, --password, --client-keys, --server-key)
364   options_builder_add_dependency_conflicts(b, "no-auth", "key",
365                                            "Cannot use --no-auth with --key (key requires authentication)");
366   options_builder_add_dependency_conflicts(b, "no-auth", "password",
367                                            "Cannot use --no-auth with --password (password requires authentication)");
368   options_builder_add_dependency_conflicts(
369       b, "no-auth", "client-keys", "Cannot use --no-auth with --client-keys (key list requires authentication)");
370   options_builder_add_dependency_conflicts(
371       b, "no-auth", "server-key", "Cannot use --no-auth with --server-key (verification requires authentication)");
372 
373   // Cannot use --key with --server-key (both server-side key options)
374   options_builder_add_dependency_conflicts(
375       b, "key", "server-key",
376       "Cannot use --key with --server-key (--key is server identity, --server-key is client-side)");
377 
378   // ============================================================================
379   // Compression Conflicts
380   // ============================================================================
381   // Cannot use --no-compress with --compression-level
382   options_builder_add_dependency_conflicts(b, "no-compress", "compression-level",
383                                            "Cannot use --no-compress with --compression-level");
384 
385   // ============================================================================
386   // Audio Encoding Conflicts
387   // ============================================================================
388   // Cannot use both --encode-audio and --no-encode-audio
389   options_builder_add_dependency_conflicts(b, "encode-audio", "no-encode-audio",
390                                            "Cannot use both --encode-audio and --no-encode-audio");
391 
392   // ============================================================================
393   // Display & Screen Conflicts
394   // ============================================================================
395 
396   // ============================================================================
397   // Requirements (dependencies that must be satisfied)
398   // ============================================================================
399   // --snapshot-delay requires --snapshot
400   options_builder_add_dependency_requires(b, "snapshot-delay", "snapshot",
401                                           "Option --snapshot-delay requires --snapshot");
402 
403   // --loop requires --file (can't loop network streams)
404   options_builder_add_dependency_requires(b, "loop", "file", "Option --loop requires --file");
405 
406   const options_config_t *config = options_builder_build(b);
407   options_builder_destroy(b);
408   return config;
409 }










void
  options_print_help_for_mode (const options_config_t * config,
  asciichat_mode_t mode, const char * program_name, const char *
  description, FILE * desc)


Print help for a specific mode or binary level (unified function).
    This is the single unified function for all help output (binary level and
    all modes). It handles all layout logic, terminal detection, and section
    printing.




Parameters


Config Module Options config with
  all options


mode Mode to show help for (-1 for binary-level help)


program_name Full program name (e.g., "ascii-chat server")


description Brief description


desc Output file stream


Print help for a specific mode or binary level (unified
  function).




This is the single unified function for all help output (binary
    level and all modes). It handles common layout logic, terminal detection,
    and section printing.




Parameters


Config Module Options config with
  all options (binary + all modes)


mode Mode to show help for (use -1 for binary-level help)


program_name Full program name with mode (e.g., "ascii-chat
  server")


description Brief description of the mode/binary


desc Output file stream (usually stdout)


Definition at line 1094 of file help.c.


1095                                                                       {
1096   if (!config || !desc) {
1097     if (desc) {
1098       fprintf(desc, "Error: Failed to create options config\n");
1099     } else {
1100       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Config or desc is NULL");
1101     }
1102     return;
1103   }
1104 
1105   // Detect terminal width early so we can decide whether to show ASCII art
1106   int term_width = 80;
1107   terminal_size_t term_size;
1108   if (terminal_get_size(&term_size) == ASCIICHAT_OK && term_size.cols > 40) {
1109     term_width = term_size.cols;
1110   } else {
1111     const char *cols_env = SAFE_GETENV("COLUMNS");
1112     if (cols_env) {
1113       int cols = atoi(cols_env);
1114       if (cols > 40)
1115         term_width = cols;
1116     }
1117   }
1118 
1119   // Print ASCII art logo only if terminal is wide enough (ASCII art is ~52 chars wide)
1120   if (term_width >= 60) {
1121     (void)fprintf(desc, "  __ _ ___  ___(_|_)       ___| |__   __ _| |_ \n");
1122     (void)fprintf(desc, " / _` / __|/ __| | |_____ / __| '_ \\ / _` | __|\n");
1123     (void)fprintf(desc, "| (_| \\__ \\ (__| | |_____| (__| | | | (_| | |_ \n");
1124     (void)fprintf(desc, " \\__,_|___/\\___|_|_|      \\___|_| |_|\\__,_|\\__|\n");
1125     (void)fprintf(desc, "\n");
1126   }
1127 
1128   // Print program name and description (color mode name magenta if it's a mode-specific help)
1129   if (program_name) {
1130     const char *space = strchr(program_name, ' ');
1131     if (space && mode >= 0) {
1132       // Mode-specific help: color the mode name
1133       int binary_len = space - program_name;
1134       (void)fprintf(desc, "%.*s %s - %s\n\n", binary_len, program_name, colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, space + 1),
1135                     description);
1136     } else {
1137       // Binary-level help: use colored_string for the program name too
1138       (void)fprintf(desc, "%s - %s\n\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, program_name), description);
1139     }
1140   }
1141 
1142   // Print project links
1143   print_project_links(desc);
1144   (void)fprintf(desc, "\n");
1145 
1146   // Determine if this is binary-level help (called for 'ascii-chat --help')
1147   // Binary help uses MODE_DISCOVERY as the mode value
1148   bool for_binary_help = (mode == MODE_DISCOVERY);
1149 
1150   // Calculate column widths for USAGE upfront (before printing) for alignment
1151   int usage_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "usage", mode, for_binary_help);
1152   // Use USAGE width for both USAGE and EXAMPLES sections for consistent alignment
1153   // (don't let long examples push column too far right)
1154   int usage_examples_col_width = usage_max_col_width;
1155 
1156   // Print USAGE section (with section-specific column width and mode filtering)
1157   fprintf(desc, "%s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, "USAGE"));
1158   if (config->num_usage_lines > 0) {
1159     // Get mode name for filtering usage lines
1160     const char *mode_name = NULL;
1161     switch (mode) {
1162     case MODE_SERVER:
1163       mode_name = "server";
1164       break;
1165     case MODE_CLIENT:
1166       mode_name = "client";
1167       break;
1168     case MODE_MIRROR:
1169       mode_name = "mirror";
1170       break;
1171     case MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE:
1172       mode_name = "discovery-service";
1173       break;
1174     case MODE_DISCOVERY:
1175       mode_name = NULL; // Binary help shows all usage lines
1176       break;
1177     default:
1178       mode_name = NULL;
1179       break;
1180     }
1181 
1182     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_usage_lines; i++) {
1183       const usage_descriptor_t *usage = &config->usage_lines[i];
1184 
1185       // Filter usage lines by mode
1186       if (!for_binary_help) {
1187         // For mode-specific help, show the current mode's usage line and the generic [mode] --help line
1188         bool is_current_mode = (usage->mode && mode_name && strcmp(usage->mode, mode_name) == 0);
1189         bool is_generic_help_line =
1190             (!usage->mode && usage->positional && strcmp(usage->positional, "[mode] --help") == 0);
1191 
1192         if (!is_current_mode && !is_generic_help_line) {
1193           continue;
1194         }
1195       }
1196 
1197       char usage_buf[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM];
1198       int len = 0;
1199 
1200       len += safe_snprintf(usage_buf + len, sizeof(usage_buf) - len, "ascii-chat");
1201 
1202       // For mode-specific help, use [mode] placeholder when showing --help usage
1203       if (usage->mode) {
1204         if (!for_binary_help && usage->positional && strcmp(usage->positional, "--help") == 0) {
1205           // Mode-specific help: show [mode] --help as generic placeholder
1206           len +=
1207               safe_snprintf(usage_buf + len, sizeof(usage_buf) - len, " %s", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "[mode]"));
1208         } else {
1209           // Binary help or non-help usage: show actual mode name
1210           len += safe_snprintf(usage_buf + len, sizeof(usage_buf) - len, " %s",
1211                                colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, usage->mode));
1212         }
1213       }
1214 
1215       if (usage->positional) {
1216         len += safe_snprintf(usage_buf + len, sizeof(usage_buf) - len, " %s",
1217                              colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, usage->positional));
1218       }
1219 
1220       if (usage->show_options) {
1221         const char *options_text =
1222             (usage->mode && strcmp(usage->mode, "<mode>") == 0) ? "[mode-options...]" : "[options...]";
1223         len += safe_snprintf(usage_buf + len, sizeof(usage_buf) - len, " %s",
1224                              colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, options_text));
1225       }
1226 
1227       layout_print_two_column_row(desc, usage_buf, usage->description, usage_examples_col_width, term_width, 0);
1228     }
1229   }
1230   fprintf(desc, "\n");
1231 
1232   // Print MODES section (only for binary-level help)
1233   if (for_binary_help && config->num_modes > 0) {
1234     int modes_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "modes", mode, for_binary_help);
1235     print_modes_section(config, desc, term_width, modes_max_col_width);
1236   }
1237 
1238   // Print positional argument examples (with mode filtering and section-specific column width)
1239   if (config->num_positional_args > 0) {
1240     // First, check if any positional args apply to this mode
1241     option_mode_bitmask_t current_mode_bitmask = 1U << mode;
1242     bool has_applicable_positional_args = false;
1243 
1244     for (size_t pa_idx = 0; pa_idx < config->num_positional_args; pa_idx++) {
1245       const positional_arg_descriptor_t *pos_arg = &config->positional_args[pa_idx];
1246 
1247       // Filter by mode_bitmask (matching the parsing code logic)
1248       if (pos_arg->mode_bitmask != 0 && !(pos_arg->mode_bitmask & current_mode_bitmask)) {
1249         continue;
1250       }
1251 
1252       if (pos_arg->section_heading && pos_arg->examples && pos_arg->num_examples > 0) {
1253         has_applicable_positional_args = true;
1254         break;
1255       }
1256     }
1257 
1258     // Only print the section if there are applicable positional args
1259     if (has_applicable_positional_args) {
1260       int positional_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "positional", mode, false);
1261 
1262       for (size_t pa_idx = 0; pa_idx < config->num_positional_args; pa_idx++) {
1263         const positional_arg_descriptor_t *pos_arg = &config->positional_args[pa_idx];
1264 
1265         // Filter by mode_bitmask (matching the parsing code logic)
1266         if (pos_arg->mode_bitmask != 0 && !(pos_arg->mode_bitmask & current_mode_bitmask)) {
1267           continue;
1268         }
1269 
1270         if (pos_arg->section_heading && pos_arg->examples && pos_arg->num_examples > 0) {
1271           (void)fprintf(desc, "%s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, pos_arg->section_heading));
1272 
1273           for (size_t i = 0; i < pos_arg->num_examples; i++) {
1274             const char *example = pos_arg->examples[i];
1275             const char *p = example;
1276             const char *desc_start = NULL;
1277 
1278             while (*p == ' ')
1279               p++;
1280             const char *first_part = p;
1281 
1282             while (*p && !(*p == ' ' && *(p + 1) == ' '))
1283               p++;
1284             int first_len_bytes = (int)(p - first_part);
1285 
1286             while (*p == ' ')
1287               p++;
1288             if (*p) {
1289               desc_start = p;
1290             }
1291 
1292             char colored_first_part[256];
1293             safe_snprintf(colored_first_part, sizeof(colored_first_part), "%.*s", first_len_bytes, first_part);
1294             char colored_result[512];
1295             safe_snprintf(colored_result, sizeof(colored_result), "%s",
1296                           colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, colored_first_part));
1297 
1298             layout_print_two_column_row(desc, colored_result, desc_start ? desc_start : "", positional_max_col_width,
1299                                         term_width, 0);
1300           }
1301           (void)fprintf(desc, "\n");
1302         }
1303       }
1304     }
1305   }
1306 
1307   // Print EXAMPLES section (using USAGE column width for alignment)
1308   print_examples_section(config, desc, term_width, usage_examples_col_width, mode, for_binary_help);
1309 
1310   // Print custom sections (after EXAMPLES, before OPTIONS)
1311   if (config->num_custom_sections > 0) {
1312     option_mode_bitmask_t current_mode_bitmask = 1U << mode;
1313     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_custom_sections; i++) {
1314       const custom_section_descriptor_t *section = &config->custom_sections[i];
1315 
1316       // Filter by mode_bitmask
1317       if (section->mode_bitmask != 0 && !(section->mode_bitmask & current_mode_bitmask)) {
1318         continue;
1319       }
1320 
1321       if (section->heading) {
1322         fprintf(desc, "%s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, section->heading));
1323       }
1324 
1325       if (section->content) {
1326         // Special handling for KEYBINDINGS section: colorize keybindings and wrap to 70 chars
1327         if (section->heading && strcmp(section->heading, "KEYBINDINGS") == 0) {
1328           // Build colored version with proper keybinding colorization
1329           char colored_output[2048] = "";
1330           const char *src = section->content;
1331           char *dst = colored_output;
1332           size_t remaining = sizeof(colored_output) - 1;
1333 
1334           while (*src && remaining > 0) {
1335             // Colorize ? that don't end sentences
1336             if (*src == '?' && *(src + 1) != '\n' && *(src + 1) != '\0') {
1337               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "?");
1338               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1339               if (len <= remaining) {
1340                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1341                 dst += len;
1342                 remaining -= len;
1343                 src += 1;
1344               } else {
1345                 break;
1346               }
1347             } else if (strncmp(src, "Space", 5) == 0 &&
1348                        (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' '))) {
1349               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "Space");
1350               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1351               if (len <= remaining) {
1352                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1353                 dst += len;
1354                 remaining -= len;
1355                 src += 5;
1356               } else {
1357                 break;
1358               }
1359             } else if (strncmp(src, "arrows", 6) == 0 &&
1360                        (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' '))) {
1361               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "arrows");
1362               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1363               if (len <= remaining) {
1364                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1365                 dst += len;
1366                 remaining -= len;
1367                 src += 6;
1368               } else {
1369                 break;
1370               }
1371             } else if (*src == 'm' && (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' ')) &&
1372                        (*(src + 1) == ',' || *(src + 1) == ')')) {
1373               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "m");
1374               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1375               if (len <= remaining) {
1376                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1377                 dst += len;
1378                 remaining -= len;
1379                 src += 1;
1380               } else {
1381                 break;
1382               }
1383             } else if (*src == 'c' && (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' ')) &&
1384                        (*(src + 1) == ',' || *(src + 1) == ')')) {
1385               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "c");
1386               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1387               if (len <= remaining) {
1388                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1389                 dst += len;
1390                 remaining -= len;
1391                 src += 1;
1392               } else {
1393                 break;
1394               }
1395             } else if (*src == 'f' && (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' ')) &&
1396                        (*(src + 1) == ',' || *(src + 1) == ')')) {
1397               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "f");
1398               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1399               if (len <= remaining) {
1400                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1401                 dst += len;
1402                 remaining -= len;
1403                 src += 1;
1404               } else {
1405                 break;
1406               }
1407             } else if (*src == 'r' && (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' ')) &&
1408                        (*(src + 1) == ')')) {
1409               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "r");
1410               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1411               if (len <= remaining) {
1412                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1413                 dst += len;
1414                 remaining -= len;
1415                 src += 1;
1416               } else {
1417                 break;
1418               }
1419             } else {
1420               *dst++ = *src++;
1421               remaining--;
1422             }
1423           }
1424           *dst = '\0';
1425           // Print with 2-space indent, wrapping at min(terminal width, 90)
1426           fprintf(desc, "  ");
1427           int keybindings_wrap_width = term_width < 90 ? term_width : 90;
1428           layout_print_wrapped_description(desc, colored_output, 2, keybindings_wrap_width, 0);
1429           fprintf(desc, "\n");
1430         } else {
1431           fprintf(desc, "%s\n", section->content);
1432         }
1433       }
1434 
1435       fprintf(desc, "\n");
1436     }
1437   }
1438 
1439   // Print options sections (with section-specific column width for options)
1440   int options_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "options", mode, for_binary_help);
1441   options_config_print_options_sections_with_width(config, desc, options_max_col_width, mode);
1442 }

Referenced by usage().






void
  options_struct_destroy (const options_config_t * config, void *
  options_struct)


Clean up memory owned by options struct. Frees all auto-duplicated
    strings and sets pointers to NULL. Call this before freeing the options
    struct.




Parameters


Config Module Options
  configuration


options_struct Options struct to clean up


Definition at line 1444 of file help.c.


1444                                                                                   {
1445   if (!config || !options_struct)
1446     return;
1447 
1448   // Free all owned strings
1449   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_owned_strings; i++) {
1450     free(config->owned_strings[i]);
1451   }
1452 
1453   // Reset owned strings tracking
1454   ((options_config_t *)config)->num_owned_strings = 0;
1455 
1456   // NULL out string fields
1457   char *base = (char *)options_struct;
1458   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1459     const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
1460     if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_STRING && desc->owns_memory) {
1461       char **field = (char **)(base + desc->offset);
1462       *field = NULL;
1463     }
1464   }
1465 }
